agenda: worktree contents: what is stripped vs kept
reviewed-state identity + the dispatcher 20s Stop budget
architecture-core.decision: Worktree-based context delivery: reviewer runs in a git-tree worktree of the project minus the methodology's KNOWN deployed machinery (.specrew/.specify/.squad + deployed scripts/internal/continuous-co-review,agent-tasks; node_modules/build auto-excluded as gitignored), reads .review/changes.diff as the entry point + design context as files, slim prompt, browses real files. ALL heavy work moves to a DETACHED async process with a generous budget; the Stop hook only triggers (<1s) so 20s never bounds the review. Nothing out of scope by fiat.

agenda: which components change vs survive vs new vs removed
component-design.decision: Change-impact map agreed: back half (reap, blackboard, signoff-gate, host adapters, selection/authorization) SURVIVES; navigator Stop-path shrinks to a trigger; worktree-materialize/digest/diff-provider/prompt-composer/reviewer-instruction/execution-engine CHANGE; NEW = detached prepare+review orchestrator + .review/ assembler; REMOVED = scaffolding-exclusion heuristic + diff cap/input-guard. ADDITIONS: manual /specrew-review (specrew-review.ps1 + skill) is a 2nd door into the SAME detached pipeline (deploy fixed); the result-delivery + human-gated status ('continue'/status) loop is KEPT + central, driving the agent to fix after a blocking verdict; review runs up to N rounds then escalates, N = co_review_max_rounds (default 2, configurable, not hardcoded).

security-compliance.decision: Reviewer is a fully-trusted component: SEES ALL (no confidentiality blinding) and RUNS ALL (agentic - executes tests/builds/exploration in the ephemeral worktree to verify) in a read-only-source ephemeral worktree; the ONLY restriction is it CANNOT FIX the code (FindingsResult not patches; real repo never mutated; worktree discarded; fixes via the result-delivery/round loop - closes G2). Confidentiality applies only to durable artifacts (redact secret values to location; no ambient/machine state). Methodology-machinery strip is relevance (review the user project, not the deployed tool), orthogonal to see-all. Strengthens F-197 trust boundary with the run capability.
security-compliance.diagram: iterations/008/workshop/security-compliance.md - reviewer trust boundary (see-all/run-all/cant-fix, read-only ephemeral worktree)

code-implementation.decision: Reuse F-197 code-rules (PowerShell 7.x idioms, Set-StrictMode, fail-open contracts, established CCR conventions); NO new dependencies (git, claude/codex CLIs, Pester 5.5 - all existing). iter-008 deltas: agentic reviewer invocation (claude -p/codex with read+run tools in the worktree cwd, slim prompt); worktree lifecycle must dispose reliably (the ~75 leftover specrew-itask-* debris); tests on Pester 5.5 + the deploy-completeness smoke-test pattern (49f88717) as the acceptance bar (deployed-and-fires, not unit-green).
